title: regression issue in command push and commit
body:
There are regression problems between command push and commit, I guess it's introduced by the fixing of #474.

1. Tested with buildah-0.12-1.git8badcc2.fc27.x86_64

```

```


comments.body: Thanks for the report @gouyang.  @ripcurld do you have time to take a look at this?
Yes, I will be working on this in the next two days.

I was reproduced on Fedora 27 with all packages are latest. 

After some investigation, it seems to be an issue between buildah and docker-distribution, no this issue if install a lower docker-distribution version on Fedora 27.
1. Test it with docker-distribution-2.5.1-1.fc26.x86_64.rpm, no this issue. 
2. Test it with docker-distribution-2.6.2-5.git48294d9.fc27.x86_64, the problem is reproduced.
@mtrmac any idea?

I am afraid it's not related. On the machine where I tried to reproduce it, I am using that docker-distribution version and still don't get the error.
```
# dnf info docker-distribution

URL          : https://github.com/docker/distribution                        
License      : ASL 2.0                                                       
Description  : Docker toolset to pack, ship, store, and deliver content
```

From reading the error message, it seems like `buildah` couldn't commit or push because it couldn't find/read the containers information stored in `/var/lib/containers/storage/overlay`:

```
error creating overlay mount to /var/lib/containers/storage/overlay/014cf8bfcb2d50b7b519c4714ac716cda5b660eae34189593ad880dc72ba4526/merged: no such file or directory
```

> @mtrmac any idea?

I don’t know nearly enough about overlay to understand those messages.  As for the `manifest unknown`s, I can’t see anything obviously wrong, .  `--debug` output, both from the publishing and consumption steps, might be useful to narrow this down a bit.

I had reproduced the issue on two Fedora 27 system, there is nothing specify settings.

Inspecting the underlying storage, _storing_ the manifest works fine, although I haven’t tracked down the reported error yet.

The problem is
```
"errors verifying manifest: unrecognized manifest content type " 
```
(note the trailing space, it is from `… type %s`)

This is very likely https://github.com/docker/distribution/pull/2076#issuecomment-328655346 : the manifests created by `containers/image`, and apparently `buildah`, don’t contain the `mediaType` field, and when the docker/distribution server processes a `GET` of a manifest, and that field is missing, the operation fails.

This should be fixed in `docker/distribution`, probably as suggested in the linked comment.

docker/distribution changes suggested in https://github.com/docker/distribution/pull/2076#issuecomment-372410481 . @owtaylor something like this could be incorporated into the Fedora packages.

This fixes reading OCI manifests, as well as the `error dispatching manifest {push, pull} to listener: missing signature key` errors (which are caused by `ocimanifest.DeserializedManifest.Payload` returning an empty MIME type, causing later parses of the returned payload to fail).
@mtrmac Should buildah do something to make sure that field is populated.

> @mtrmac Should buildah do something to make sure that field is populated.

Technically it can, but I don’t think it _should_: At this point we are more or less solidifying the de-facto spec.

So, either the spec stands, and the docker/distribution PR + the Fedora patched package should be updated to match, or the spec should be officially modified, and then `buildah` and c/image can freely add the field.

If Fedora right now patched both the docker/distribution server and the clients to rely on `mediaType` being populated, that would create a de-facto spec different from the official one. Given how young the standard is, that seems pretty bad for adoption of the standard.

If a short-term fix were needed right now, I’d suggest disabling the OCI support patches in the Fedora `docker-distribution` package, because they are not ready enough.

I don't see any problem with adding something like Mike Brown's patch
(which corresponds to my suggested fix) to the Fedora packages - I think
it's *good* to get testing on this code and find problems like this. When I
get a chance over the next day or two I'll review his patch, and look into
extending it to cover image indexes and give it some testing.
